questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Given a regular tetrahdedron OABC with side length 1 unit. Let D \& E are mid points of AB \& OC respectively. If $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{DE}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{AC}}=\frac{\mathrm{m}}{\mathrm{n}}$ (where m \& n are coprime), then $(\mathrm{m}+\mathrm{n})$ is
questions.solution:
Let $\mathrm{O}(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{O}}), \mathrm{A}(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}), \mathrm{B}(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}})$ and $\mathrm{C}(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}})$
$D$ is $\frac{\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}}{2}$ and E is $\frac{\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}}{2}$
$\overrightarrow{\mathrm{DE}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{AC}}=\left(\frac{\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}-(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}})}{2}\right) \cdot(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}-\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}})$
$=\frac{|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}|^2}{2}-\frac{\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}}{2}-\frac{\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}}{2}-\frac{\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}}{2}+\frac{|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}|^2}{2}+\frac{\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}}{2}$
$=\frac{1-\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)-\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)-\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)+1+\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)}{2}=\frac{1}{2}$
$\because$ angle between adjacent sides is $\frac{\pi}{3}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In $\triangle A B C$, a point $P$ is chosen on side $\overrightarrow{A B}$ so that $A P: P B=1: 4$ and a point $Q$ is chosen on the side $\overrightarrow{B C}$ so that $C Q: Q B=1: 3$. Segment $\overrightarrow{C P}$ and $\overrightarrow{A Q}$ intersect at $M$. If the ratio $\frac{M C}{P C}$ is expressed as a rational number in the lowest term as $\frac{\mathrm{a}}{\mathrm{b}}$, then find $(\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b})$.
questions.solution:
Vector equation of $A Q$ is $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}_1=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+\lambda\left(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}-\frac{3 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}}{4}\right)$ and vector equation of $C P$ is $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}_2=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}+\mu\left(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}-\frac{4 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}}{5}\right)$ Hence $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}_1=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}_2$ gives, $\left(1+\lambda+\frac{4 \mu}{5}\right) \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}=\left(1+\mu+\frac{3 \lambda}{4}\right) \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}$
$\therefore 1+\lambda+\frac{4 \mu}{5}=0$ and $1+\mu+\frac{3 \lambda}{4}=0$ Solving, $\lambda=-\frac{1}{2}$ and $\mu=-\frac{5}{8}$ $\begin{aligned} \therefore & \text { p.v. of } M=\vec{a}-\frac{1}{2} \vec{a}+\frac{3 \vec{c}}{8}=\frac{4 \vec{a}+3 \vec{c}}{8} \\ & \overrightarrow{M C}=\vec{c}-\frac{4 \vec{a}+3 \vec{c}}{8}=\frac{5 \vec{c}-4 \vec{a}}{8} \end{aligned}$ And $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{PC}}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}-\frac{4 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}}{5}=\frac{5 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}-4 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}}{5}$ $\begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad \frac{M C}{P C}=\frac{5}{8} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad a=5 \text { and } b=8 ; \\ & \therefore \quad a+b=13 \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Let $\vec{a}=-3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}, \vec{b}=4 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}$ and $\vec{c}=2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}$. If $V_1$ is the volume of parallelopiped whose three coterminous edges are the vectors $\vec{a}+\vec{b}, \vec{b}+\vec{c}, \vec{c}+\vec{a}$ and $V_2$ is the volume of tetrahedron whose three coterminous edges are the vectors $\vec{a} \times \vec{b}, \vec{b} \times \vec{c}, \vec{c} \times \vec{a}$, then find the value of $\left(V_1+V_2\right)$.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: OABC is a tetrahedron in which O is the origin and position vector of points $\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{B}, \mathrm{C}$ are $\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}, \quad 2 \hat{i}+\alpha \hat{j}+\hat{k}$ and $\hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}$ respectively. An integral value of $\alpha$ for which shortest distance between $O A$ and $B C$ is $\sqrt{\frac{3}{2}}$ is _______
questions.solution:
Unit vector ( $\hat{n}$ ) perpendicular to $\overrightarrow{O A}$ and $\overrightarrow{C B}$
$\hat{n}=\frac{\overrightarrow{\mathrm{CB}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{OA}}}{|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{CB}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{OA}}|}=\frac{(3 \alpha-7) \hat{\mathrm{i}}-4 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+(5-\alpha) \hat{\mathrm{k}}}{\sqrt{(3 \alpha-7)^2+16+(5-\alpha)^2}}$
As $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{CB}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{OA}}=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}\hat{\mathrm{i}} & \hat{\mathrm{j}} & \hat{\mathrm{k}} \\ 1 & \alpha-3 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & 3\end{array}\right|$
$=|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BA}} \cdot \hat{\mathrm{n}}|=\left|\frac{(7-3 \alpha)-4(2-\alpha)+2(5-\alpha)}{\sqrt{(3 \alpha-7)^2+16+(5+\alpha)^2}}\right|$
Shortest distance $=|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BA}} \cdot \vec{n}|$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $A_1, A_2, A_3, A_4$ be the areas of the triangular faces of a tetrahedron, and $h_1, h_2, h_3, h_4$ be the corresponding altitudes of the tetrahedron. If volume of tetrahedron is 5 cubic units, then find the minimum value of $\left(A_1+A_2+A_3+A_4\right)\left(h_1+h_2+h_3+h_4\right)$ (in cubic units).
questions.solution:
Volume $(V)=\frac{1}{3} A_1 h_1 \quad \Rightarrow h_1=\frac{3 V}{A_1}$
Similarly $\mathrm{h}_2=\frac{3 \mathrm{~V}}{\mathrm{~A}_2}, \mathrm{~h}_3=\frac{3 \mathrm{~V}}{\mathrm{~A}_3}$ and $\mathrm{h}_4=\frac{3 \mathrm{~V}}{\mathrm{~A}_4}$
$S o\left(A_1+A_2+A_3+A_4\right)\left(h_1+h_2+h_3+h_4\right)=\left(A_1+A_2+A_3+A_4\right)\left(\frac{3 V}{A_1}+\frac{3 V}{A_2}+\frac{3 V}{A_3}+\frac{3 V}{A_4}\right)$
$=3 V\left(A_1+A_2+A_3+A_4\right)\left(\frac{1}{A_1}+\frac{1}{A_2}+\frac{1}{A_3}+\frac{1}{A_4}\right)$

Now using A.M.-H.M inequality in $\mathrm{A}_1, \mathrm{~A}_2, \mathrm{~A}_3, \mathrm{~A}_4$, we get
$\frac{\mathrm{A}_1+\mathrm{A}_2+\mathrm{A}_3+\mathrm{A}_4}{4} \geq \frac{4}{\left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{~A}_1}+\frac{1}{\mathrm{~A}_2}+\frac{1}{\mathrm{~A}_3}+\frac{1}{\mathrm{~A}_4}\right)}$
$\Rightarrow\left(\mathrm{A}_1+\mathrm{A}_2+\mathrm{A}_3+\mathrm{A}_4\right)\left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{~A}_1}+\frac{1}{\mathrm{~A}_2}+\frac{1}{\mathrm{~A}_3}+\frac{1}{\mathrm{~A}_4}\right) \geq 16$
Hence the minimum value of $\left(\mathrm{A}_1+\mathrm{A}_2+\mathrm{A}_3+\mathrm{A}_4\right)\left(\mathrm{h}_1+\mathrm{h}_2+\mathrm{h}_3+\mathrm{h}_4\right)=3 \mathrm{~V}(16)=48 \mathrm{~V}=48 \times 5=240$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $O$ be the interior point of $\triangle A B C$ such that $2 \overrightarrow{O A}+3 \overrightarrow{O B}+6 \overrightarrow{O C}=0$ where $O$ is origin. If $\frac{\text { Area of }(\triangle A B C)}{\text { Area of }(\triangle A O B)}=\frac{m}{n}$, where $m$ and $n$ are relatively prime, then $(m-n)$ is equal to $\qquad$
questions.solution:
Area of $\triangle A B C=\frac{1}{2}|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}+\vec{b} \times \vec{c}+\vec{c} \times \vec{a}|$
Now given $2 \vec{a}+3 \vec{b}+6 \vec{c}=0$
Cross with $\vec{a}, 3 \vec{a} \times \vec{b}+6 \vec{a} \times \vec{c}=0$
or $\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=2(\vec{c} \times \vec{a}).....(i)$
Again cross with $\vec{b}$
$2 \vec{a} \times \vec{b}+6 \vec{c} \times \vec{b}=0$
or $\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=3(\vec{b} \times \vec{c})$
Area of $\Delta \mathrm{OAB}=\frac{1}{2}|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|$
$\frac{\text { Area of } \triangle \mathrm{ABC}}{\text { Area of } \triangle \mathrm{AOB}}=\frac{\frac{1}{2}|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}|\left(1+\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{3}\right)}{\frac{1}{2}|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}|}=\frac{11}{6}$
So $m=11, n=6 \Rightarrow(m-n)=5$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $O$ be the interior point of $\triangle A B C$ such that $2 O \vec{A}+3 O \vec{B}+6 \overrightarrow{O C}=0$ where $O$ is origin. If $\frac{\text { Area of }(\triangle A B C)}{\text { Area of }(\triangle A O B)}=\frac{m}{n}$, where $m$ and $n$ are relatively prime, then $(m-n)$ is equal to $\qquad$
questions.solution:
Area of $\triangle A B C=\frac{1}{2}|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}+\vec{b} \times \vec{c}+\vec{c} \times \vec{a}|$
Now given $2 \vec{a}+3 \vec{b}+6 \vec{c}=0$
Cross with $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{a}}, 3 \overline{\mathbf{a}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathbf{b}}+6 \overrightarrow{\mathbf{a}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}}=0$
or $\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=2(\vec{c} \times \vec{a})....(1)$
Again cross with $\vec{b}$
$2 \vec{a} \times \vec{b}+6 \vec{c} \times \vec{b}=0$
or $\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=3(\vec{b} \times \vec{c})$
Area of $\triangle \mathrm{OAB}=\frac{1}{2}|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|$
$\frac{\text { Arca of } \triangle \mathrm{ABC}}{\text { Area of } \triangle \mathrm{AOB}}=\frac{\frac{1}{2}|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|\left(1+\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{3}\right)}{\frac{1}{2}|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|}=\frac{11}{6}$
So $\mathrm{m}=11 . \mathrm{n}=6$
$\Rightarrow(\mathrm{m}-\mathrm{n})=5$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
$\rightarrow \rightarrow \rightarrow$
$\mathbf{a}, \mathbf{b}, \overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}}$ are 3 non-coplanar unit vectors inclined at angle $\alpha\left(\leq \frac{\pi}{2}\right)$ to each other. If the volume of tetrahedron formed by these vectors is $\frac{1}{\sqrt{360}}$, then find the value of $10\left(3 \cos ^2 \alpha-2 \cos ^3 \alpha\right)$
questions.solution:
Given,
$$
\overrightarrow{\mathbf{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathbf{b}}=\overrightarrow{\mathbf{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}}=\overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathbf{a}}=\cos \alpha
$$
$\therefore \quad$ Volume of tetrahedron $=\frac{1}{6}\left[\begin{array}{lll}\overrightarrow{\mathbf{a}} & \overrightarrow{\mathbf{b}} & \overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}}\end{array}\right]$

Now,
$$
\left[\begin{array}{lll}
\overrightarrow{\mathbf{a}} & \overrightarrow{\mathbf{b}} & \overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}}
\end{array}\right]^2=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}
1 & \cos \alpha & \cos \alpha \\
\cos \alpha & 1 & \cos \alpha \\
\cos \alpha & \cos \alpha & 1
\end{array}\right|
$$
$\begin{aligned} 36 V^2 & =(1+2 \cos \alpha)(1-\cos \alpha)^2 \\ 36 \times \frac{1}{360} & =(1+2 \cos \alpha)(1-\cos \alpha)^2=\frac{1}{10} \\ \therefore \quad 3 \cos ^2 \alpha-2 \cos ^3 \alpha & =\frac{9}{10}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Given a tetrahedron $D-A B C$ with $A B=12, C D=6$. If the shortest distance between the skew lines $A B$ and $C D$ is 8 and the angle between them is $\frac{\pi}{6}$, then find the volume of tetrahedron.
questions.solution:
Given $|\vec{b}-\vec{a}|=12 ; \quad|\vec{c}|=6$ Equation of CD is $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}=\lambda \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}$ \& eq. of $A B$ is $\vec{r}=\vec{a}+\mu(\vec{b}-\vec{a})$ $\text { S.D. }=\left|\frac{\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c} \times(\vec{b}-\vec{a})}{\vec{c} \times(\vec{b}-\vec{a})}\right|=8$
$\begin{aligned} & \left|\frac{\left[\begin{array}{lll}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} & \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} & \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}\end{array}\right]}{\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} \times(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}-\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}})}\right|=8 \\ & \left|\left[\begin{array}{lll}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} & \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} & \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}\end{array}\right]\right|=8|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} \times(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}-\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}})| \\ & 6 \mathrm{~V}=\frac{8|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}||\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}-\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}|}{2} \\ & \Rightarrow 6 \mathrm{~V}=(4)(6)(12) \Rightarrow V=48\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q:
Vector $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{O A}}, \overrightarrow{\mathbf{O B}}, \overrightarrow{\mathbf{O C}}$ are on the same plane, $|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{O A}}|=1,|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{O B}}|=1,|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{O C}}|=\sqrt{2}$, the relative position is shown in the figure.
If $\tan \alpha=7$ and $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{O B}} \wedge \overrightarrow{\mathbf{O C}}=\frac{\pi}{4}$ and $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{O C}}=m \overrightarrow{\mathbf{O A}}+n \overrightarrow{\mathbf{O B}},(m, n \in R)$, find the value of $(m+n)$.
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
\overrightarrow{O C} & =m \overrightarrow{O A}+n \overrightarrow{O B} \\
\overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}} & =m \overrightarrow{\mathbf{a}}+n \overrightarrow{\mathbf{b}} \\
|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{a}}| & =1,|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{b}}|=1,|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}}|=\sqrt{2}, \tan \alpha=7
\end{aligned}
$$
Given
Now take dot of equation (1) with $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{a}}$ and $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}}$ to get
$$
\begin{aligned}
m & =5 / 4 ; n=7 / 4 \\
\therefore \quad m+n & =3
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{a}}, \overrightarrow{\mathbf{b}}, \overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}}$ are three vectors such that $\left\{\begin{array}{l}|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{a}}|=|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}}|=1 \\ |\overrightarrow{\mathbf{b}}|=4 \\ \overrightarrow{\mathbf{b}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}}=2 \\ \overrightarrow{2}=\overrightarrow{\mathbf{b}}=\overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}}+\lambda \mathbf{a}\end{array}\right.$ where $\lambda$ is a scalar. If the value of $\lambda$ is equal to $\sqrt{\alpha-\beta \sqrt{3}}$ where $\alpha$ and $\beta$ are natural numbers, then find the value of $\alpha+\beta$.
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \text { Given } \quad|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{b}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}}|=2 \\
& |\overrightarrow{\mathbf{b}}||\overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}}| \sin \theta=2 \Rightarrow \sin \theta=\frac{1}{2} \\
& \therefore \quad \theta=30^{\circ} \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$

Hence, angle between $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{b}}$ and $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}}$ is $30^{\circ}$.
Now, $\quad 2 \overrightarrow{\mathbf{b}}-\overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}}=\lambda \overrightarrow{\mathbf{a}} \Rightarrow|2 \overrightarrow{\mathbf{b}}-\overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}}|=|\lambda \overrightarrow{\mathbf{a}}| \Rightarrow|2 \overrightarrow{\mathbf{b}}-\overrightarrow{\mathbf{c}}|^2=\lambda^2|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{a}}|^2$
Hence,
$$
\begin{aligned}
\lambda^2 & =65-8 \sqrt{3} \\
\lambda & =\sqrt{65-8 \sqrt{3}} \equiv \sqrt{\alpha-\beta \sqrt{3}}
\end{aligned}
$$

Hence,
$$
\alpha=65 \text { and } \beta=8
$$
$$
\therefore \quad \alpha+\beta=73
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
